metadata.title: The Documentation Doctrine
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Her superpower is turning messy, unstructured work into reliable reporting tables and documentation. She built data pipelines and dashboards that made ambiguous work legible. The doctrine demands the actual do/don't list and the explicit decision tree, not vague guidance. Six deliverables with serial dependencies need documentation so the chain never stalls.

artifactLines: Her superpower is turning messy, unstructured work into reliable reporting tables and documentation.
She built data pipelines and dashboards that made ambiguous work legible.
The doctrine demands the actual do/don't list and the explicit decision tree, not vague guidance.
Six deliverables with serial dependencies need documentation so the chain never stalls.

beats.narration_text: The move that grabbed me was turning messy, unstructured work into reliable tables, do/don't lists, and decision trees. I want to apply that doctrine to my own tangled project. Walk me through: (1) how to convert a vague task into an explicit do/don't list plus a decision tree, (2) how to map six deliverables with serial dependencies so nothing stalls the chain, and (3) how documentation like this compresses a two-person, multi-week process into something one person can actually run.

props.command: The move that grabbed me was turning messy, unstructured work into reliable tables, do/don't lists, and decision trees. I want to apply that doctrine to my own tangled project. Walk me through: (1) how to convert a vague task into an explicit do/don't list plus a decision tree, (2) how to map six deliverables with serial dependencies so nothing stalls the chain, and (3) how documentation like this compresses a two-person, multi-week process into something one person can actually run.
output: She has templates, prompts, exemplars, and principles
Her superpower is turning messy, unstructured work into reliable

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'The Documentation Doctrine' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'The Documentation Doctrine' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
